mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the monthly Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns turn negative when both proxies rise simultaneously for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0 percent confirms supply-side resilience, reducing input cost volatility and supporting positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises but S&P 500 forward returns remain negative in over 50% of cases, indicating supply strength does not translate to equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP above 2.0 percent per annum reflects underlying economic momentum, which sustains corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.0% but 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, breaking the growth-return linkage.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 2.5 percent annualized triggers inflation hedging demands and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPI exceeds 2.5% but S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances, or the signal shows no predictive power in a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad labor market tightening, which increases wage-driven consumption and lifts equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1200 thousand units alongside rising consumer credit balances signal a credit-fueled demand surge, which boosts cyclical equity sectors and drives positive returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts and credit rise simultaneously but S&P 500 forward returns are flat or negative in over 60% of triggered periods, indicating demand does not flow to equities.
